AC: SKILL.md (and tool where mechanical) cover: router fields, mechanical level-triggered orchestrator loop, acceptance gate before done, escalation with retry bound and explicit wreckage policy, worker exit contract, and the ephemeral episodic memory layer; doctor stays ok; ALT:easy fields land unchanged as phase 1.

Body.raw:
ALTERNATIVE (full cascade routing) design. Original todo: 557ab9d3. Minimal
counterpart: ALT:easy, todo 5774cf41 (5774cf4107ea07d652a8c87069f188d356f548cf9c9338b2f2e459cf1a9caadf) -- it is phase 1 of this design
(the router fields) and remains valid as a subset.

Design (chat discussion 2026-07-17/18):

1. Router (= ALT:easy 5774cf41): groomed execution.capability / execution.effort
   on WorkItems/subtodos set the STARTING tier; absent = inherit.

2. Orchestrator: one cheap agent (haiku/sonnet) per parent todo. Purely mechanical:
   polls work-item-read / wait-for, follows next hints, dispatches workers; never
   authors content. Judgment operations (splitting, grooming, merge-conflict
   resolution, parent synthesis) are dispatched as work items to capable tiers,
   not done inline by the orchestrator.
   Level-triggered: on ANY wake (harness completion notification, poll timeout,
   manual poke) it recomputes the frontier from the store (ls -t, Subtodos states,
   work-item-read) rather than trusting remembered launches. Wake channels are
   latency optimizations only; the orchestrator is restartable-stateless.

3. Acceptance gate: a work item / subtodo is done only when its AC / test gate
   passes, checked mechanically -- never worker self-report.

4. Escalation (the cascade half): on gate failure, re-dispatch the SAME item at
   the next tier up (sonnet -> opus -> fable), bounded retries, then userneeded.
   Wreckage policy is explicit in the escalated prompt: continue from the failed
   attempt's commits, or reset to BaseSha / last good work-item sha.

5. Worker exit contract (durable correctness floor): workers run fresh-context;
   before exit, everything needed for correctness lands in durable stores --
   commits with full -m messages, work-item-done message, ActualSummary. These
   have mechanical consumers (merge-subtodo commit subject, escalation prompt,
   observability) and must survive loss of any cache.

6. Episodic memory (recall tail, ephemeral): embedding index (redis or similar)
   over worker session transcripts for read-time retrieval; optionally include
   session n-1 verbatim in prompt n (one hop, recency continuity). Explicitly
   ephemeral: losing it must never break resumption -- the git+todo ledger stays
   the correctness floor. Caution: do not feed a FAILED session verbatim to the
   escalated worker (anchoring / context poisoning); escalation prefers the
   distilled attempt summary plus targeted retrieval.

7. Prompt assembly (orchestrator): todo prompt (WHY chain) + WHAT-state resume
   info (files touched, last-sha, failing test output; a prompt --resume style
   extension) + skill insertion + episodic retrieval hits.

Summary.raw: Todo tool: routing and agent choice ALT:complete
